sections.heading: Intro
sections.text: "Shawn and Gus in Drag (Racing)" is the fifth episode of Season Five . When a car thief turns up dead, Shawn and Gus must infiltrate a drag racing ring to find the killer. Shawn soon finds himself enjoying the fast lane too much, which not only endangers the investigation but also their lives.

sections.heading: Plot Summary
sections.text:
In 1990, Shawn and Gus sit in Henry's truck with the engine running. Shawn shifts the truck into reverse and slowly backs down the driveway. Panic sets in when he realizes he's too short to reach the brake pedal. Luckily, Henry gets home and stops Shawn before anything happens, telling Shawn that he's too young to drive.
In the present day, a Lamborghini is hijacked by a masked thief. Lassiter and Juliet get the call, and since Lassiter has been frustrated by his lack of progress breaking up a car theft ring, they decide to take the lead on the case. The car thief they were chasing ends up shot dead somewhere along the route of the chase. Now, they have a murder and a car theft ring to solve.
Back at the station, Lassiter is investigating the murder. Shawn gets a "vision" and tells Lassiter that the shooter was in a second car with wide tires and that the stolen Lamborghini had an altered VIN number, which means it was stolen before. Juliet tells everyone that the victim's name is Max Contreras, a street racer with a long list of priors. Max is also a huge gambler and owes a bunch of money to Logan Paget, a big time exotic car collector and suspected loan shark. Shawn and Gus are on the case with Henry 's blessing, as long as they stay under the radar while they snoop around the "Fast and Furious" world of street racing. Lassiter is none too pleased with Shawn and Gus's involvement on the case, as he has been focused on cracking down on street racers for some time.
Lassiter and Juliet go to question Paget, who tells them that Max worked for him occasionally as a mechanic. However, he hadn't talked to Max for weeks. Lassiter informs him that he was the registered owner of the car that Max was driving. Paget claims the car was stolen, but Lassiter and Juliet question why he never reported it stolen. Both Lassiter and Juliet know that Paget wouldn't kill someone who owned him money; otherwise, the debt would never be repaid.
Shawn and Gus show up at the garage where Max had previously worked on cars. They first question a woman named Gina, who claims to know nothing about any street racing and tells them to scram. Both Shawn and Gus can see that she's lying, as well as sporting a permanent injury to her right hand. Soon, they meet ultra-cool Tommy Nix , one of the street racers. They ask when the next race is. Tommy doesn't let on, saying that Shawn and Gus wouldn't know the location because they weren't invited. So, Shawn tracks down the location himself by using Gus as a distraction and flipping through Gina's phone for clues. Shawn knows they need to be a part of the race to gain street cred, so he "borrows" Henry's truck and adds some nitrous to it for extra speed. Shawn and Gus end up racing Tommy, and, in turn, Tommy is impressed with Shawn and Gus's "guts", and the two end up being accepted into this close-knit group.
Lassiter and Juliet question Paget again. Phone records show that he spoke with Max only a week ago, which contradicts his earlier statement. In fact, the two had talked shortly after the Lamborghini was stolen. Lassiter theorizes that Max knew who the thief was and offered to retrieve the car in exchange for his gambling debt being erased. However, Max then decided to keep the car for himself, so Paget hunted him down and killed him. Paget relents and admits that he called Max after Max retrieved the car, but the rest of the story isn't true. There was fear in Max's voice, leading Paget to think that Max knew who was after him and how the whole thing was going to end. Whoever killed Max had to have been a better driver than him, which puts the focus back on the street racing gang.
They work hard to figure out potential suspects and narrow it down to Gina, Max's ex-girlfriend. Lassiter and Juliet act on their own investigation and end up arresting Gina, whom they believe not only committed the murder, but also could lead them to the head of the car theft ring. When they get back to SBPD, ballistics shows that Gina's gun was used to kill Max. Lassiter theorizes that Gina's crew stole the Lamborghini, but Max betrayed them and stole it back. So, Gina took revenge. But, Shawn determines that she couldn't have been the shooter because she had previously lost the ability to use two of her fingers on her right hand. Since the shot that killed Max came from his left (the killer's right), there's no way Gina could grip both a gun and a steering wheel going 90 MPH. Unfortunately, this forces Shawn to face that the murderer and leader of the car theft ring is Tommy, the cool guy that Shawn has bonded with. Shawn figures out that Tommy had murdered the thief in the beginning because he was trying to go independent and start his own car theft ring, and Max was just getting in the way.
At the same moment, Tommy realizes Shawn is working for the police, and he informs Shawn that he is the one that is going to retrieve the sports car Max was driving out of police impound — noncompliance will result in harm coming to Gus. Tommy is going to use the car to get away and to finally seek the ultimate thrill: hitting a speed of 200 MPH. Shawn retrieves the car, and just as Tommy is speeding away in it, it is revealed that Shawn was clever enough to plan ahead. It turns out that Shawn placed zucchini in Tommy's tail pipe. As the police are chasing after him, and as Tommy closes in on 200 MPH, the car gives out. Shawn's "vegetables in the tail pipe" foil works. Tommy is arrested for the murder of Max and for leading the car ring, and Gina is acquitted.

sections.heading: Trivia
sections.text: There is again a play on words in the title, involving drag racing, and the phrase 'in drag', used in the theater to describe a cross-dressing male actor playing a female character, usually with no serious effort made to hide the true gender of the performer. When Shawn asks Tommy how close he got to doing 200 mph, he replies "187". This is a subtle hint that Tommy is the murderer. "187" is a slang term for murder referring to the California Penal Code section 187 which defines murder. Shawn wants to enter Gus's car into a street race, but Gus flatly rejects the idea, claiming that Shawn already tricked his car once. This is a reference to S2E4 " Zero to Murder in Sixty Seconds ". Gus's claim that he's been applying the lavender scalp oil that Shawn finds in his glove compartment since they were models is a call-back to S2E15 " Black and Tan: A Crime of Fashion ". When confronted by Tommy and his gang in the Psych office and is revealed to be a psychic detective, Shawn tries bringing up the crazy cases they've solved. He mentions a shark before being cut off by Gus, likely referencing S4E15 " The Head, the Tail, the Whole Damn Episode ". This episode marks the final appearance of Liam James as Young Shawn due to the producers believing he had gotten too old to play the role. Two episodes later (S5E7 " Ferry Tale ") Young Shawn has been recast and is now portrayed by Skyler Gisondo .
